name: Vermont (United States)
description: A blue field bearing the Vermont coat of arms in the center: a landscape with a tall pine tree on a green hill, flanked by three sheaves of wheat and a red cow against a backdrop of the Green Mountains, surmounted by a buck's head and crossed pine boughs. Below is a red scroll inscribed 'Vermont — Freedom and Unity'.
history: Adopted on 1 June 1923. It is Vermont's third state flag. The first (1804) was modeled on the U.S. flag with the addition of 'Vermont' on the upper red stripe; the second (1837) showed an arms-on-blue design that resembled the U.S. flag too closely when carried by Vermont regiments in the Civil War. The 1923 redesign placed the full state coat of arms — first adopted in 1821 — on a plain blue field to make Vermont's regimental colors clearly distinguishable.

features.role: the coat of arms of Vermont
features.symbolism: depicts a single tall pine tree on a green hill, flanked by sheaves of grain and a red cow representing Vermont's agriculture and dairy industry, with the Green Mountains rising in the background; below, a red ribbon with the state motto 'Freedom and Unity' and the name 'Vermont'; above, a deer's head and crossed pine boughs
